questions.text:
Choose the word that best fits the blank:
Large multinational banks have become financial _____, influencing global markets.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Behemoth** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** A huge or powerful entity; something enormous and overwhelmingly strong.
**Hindi Meaning:** विशालकाय, अत्यंत शक्तिशाली संस्था
**Synonyms:** Giant, colossus, titan, leviathan, powerhouse
**Antonyms:** Dwarf, small entity, minor player
**Example:** Large multinational banks have become financial behemoths influencing global markets.

**Option 0: dwarf**
**English Meaning:** Something very small or weak.
**Hindi Meaning:** बौना
**Synonyms:** Minor player
**Antonyms:** Behemoth
**Example:** The startup remained a dwarf compared to giants.

**Option 1: small entity**
**English Meaning:** A weak or insignificant organisation.
**Hindi Meaning:** छोटी इकाई
**Synonyms:** Underdog
**Antonyms:** Behemoth
**Example:** The small entity could not compete.

**Option 2: behemoth** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** A huge or powerful entity; something enormous and overwhelmingly strong.
**Hindi Meaning:** विशालकाय, अत्यंत शक्तिशाली संस्था
**Synonyms:** Colossus, titan
**Antonyms:** Dwarf, minor player
**Example:** Large multinational banks have become financial behemoths influencing global markets.

**Option 3: minor player**
**English Meaning:** An unimportant participant.
**Hindi Meaning:** छोटा खिलाड़ी
**Synonyms:** Small entity
**Antonyms:** Behemoth
**Example:** It was just a minor player in the industry.

**Option 4: underdog**
**English Meaning:** Expected to lose.
**Hindi Meaning:** कमजोर पक्ष
**Synonyms:** Dwarf
**Antonyms:** Behemoth
**Example:** The underdog surprised everyone.

The sentence describes an enormously powerful force. Only 'behemoth' fits.

questions.text: Choose the closest synonym for 'Compendium':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Compendium** (the correct synonym is 'concise summary and digest')

**English Meaning:** A concise collection of detailed information; a short but complete summary.
**Hindi Meaning:** संक्षिप्त संग्रह, सार-संग्रह
**Synonyms:** Summary, digest, anthology, compilation, handbook
**Antonyms:** Expansion, elaboration, disorganization
**Example:** The report serves as a compendium of key economic policies implemented over the decade.

**Option 0: expansion and elaboration**
**English Meaning:** Making something longer and more detailed.
**Hindi Meaning:** विस्तार और विस्तृत विवरण
**Synonyms:** Elaboration
**Antonyms:** Compendium
**Example:** The book was an expansion and elaboration of the idea.

**Option 1: disorganization and chaos**
**English Meaning:** Lack of order.
**Hindi Meaning:** अव्यवस्था और अराजकता
**Synonyms:** Disorganization
**Antonyms:** Compendium
**Example:** The notes were full of disorganization and chaos.

**Option 2: concise summary and digest** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** A concise collection of detailed information; a short but complete summary.
**Hindi Meaning:** संक्षिप्त संग्रह, सार-संग्रह
**Synonyms:** Anthology, handbook
**Antonyms:** Expansion, disorganization
**Example:** The report serves as a compendium of key economic policies implemented over the decade.

**Option 3: detailed expansion**
**English Meaning:** Full and lengthy explanation.
**Hindi Meaning:** विस्तृत विस्तार
**Synonyms:** Elaboration
**Antonyms:** Compendium
**Example:** He gave a detailed expansion of the topic.

**Option 4: lengthy explanation**
**English Meaning:** Long and detailed account.
**Hindi Meaning:** लंबा विवरण
**Synonyms:** Expansion
**Antonyms:** Compendium
**Example:** The teacher gave a lengthy explanation.

The word means a short, complete collection. 'Concise summary and digest' is the closest synonym.

questions.text: Choose the antonym of 'Ameliorate':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Ameliorate**

**English Meaning:** To make a situation better; to improve something that is unsatisfactory.
**Hindi Meaning:** सुधार करना, बेहतर बनाना
**Synonyms:** Improve, alleviate, enhance, better, upgrade
**Antonyms:** Worsen, aggravate, deteriorate
**Example:** Targeted reforms were introduced to ameliorate the conditions of marginalized communities.

**Option 0: improve**
**English Meaning:** To make better.
**Hindi Meaning:** सुधारना
**Synonyms:** Enhance
**Antonyms:** Worsen
**Example:** The plan will improve living conditions.

**Option 1: alleviate**
**English Meaning:** To reduce suffering.
**Hindi Meaning:** कम करना
**Synonyms:** Upgrade
**Antonyms:** Worsen
**Example:** Medicines alleviate pain.

**Option 2: worsen** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** To make something worse.
**Hindi Meaning:** बिगाड़ना
**Synonyms:** Aggravate, deteriorate
**Antonyms:** Ameliorate
**Example:** Targeted reforms were introduced to ameliorate the conditions of marginalized communities.

**Option 3: enhance**
**English Meaning:** To increase quality.
**Hindi Meaning:** बढ़ाना
**Synonyms:** Improve
**Antonyms:** Worsen
**Example:** The training will enhance skills.

**Option 4: upgrade**
**English Meaning:** To improve to a higher level.
**Hindi Meaning:** उन्नत करना
**Synonyms:** Enhance
**Antonyms:** Worsen
**Example:** They decided to upgrade the software.

The question asks for the antonym. 'Worsen' is the opposite of 'Ameliorate'.

questions.text:
Choose the word that best fits the blank:
Consumers’ _____ to save declined during periods of economic uncertainty.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Propensity** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** A natural tendency or inclination to behave in a particular way.
**Hindi Meaning:** प्रवृत्ति, झुकाव
**Synonyms:** Tendency, inclination, disposition, leaning, penchant
**Antonyms:** Aversion, disinclination, reluctance
**Example:** Consumers’ propensity to save declined during periods of economic uncertainty.

**Option 0: aversion**
**English Meaning:** Strong dislike.
**Hindi Meaning:** घृणा
**Synonyms:** Disinclination
**Antonyms:** Propensity
**Example:** He has an aversion to risk.

**Option 1: disinclination**
**English Meaning:** Lack of willingness.
**Hindi Meaning:** अनिच्छा
**Synonyms:** Reluctance
**Antonyms:** Propensity
**Example:** She showed disinclination to join.

**Option 2: propensity** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** A natural tendency or inclination to behave in a particular way.
**Hindi Meaning:** प्रवृत्ति, झुकाव
**Synonyms:** Inclination, leaning
**Antonyms:** Aversion, reluctance
**Example:** Consumers’ propensity to save declined during periods of economic uncertainty.

**Option 3: reluctance**
**English Meaning:** Unwillingness.
**Hindi Meaning:** अनिच्छा
**Synonyms:** Disinclination
**Antonyms:** Propensity
**Example:** His reluctance was obvious.

**Option 4: hesitation**
**English Meaning:** Uncertainty before acting.
**Hindi Meaning:** हिचक
**Synonyms:** Reluctance
**Antonyms:** Propensity
**Example:** There was hesitation in the decision.

The sentence refers to a natural tendency. Only 'propensity' fits.

questions.text: Choose the closest synonym for 'Adhocracy':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Adhocracy** (the correct synonym is 'flexibility and informality')

**English Meaning:** A flexible and informal system of management that encourages quick decisions and innovation.
**Hindi Meaning:** अव्यवस्थित संगठन, लचीली कार्यप्रणाली
**Synonyms:** Flexibility, informality, decentralization, fluidity
**Antonyms:** Bureaucracy, rigidity, hierarchy
**Example:** Startups often thrive in an adhocracy that encourages innovation and quick decision-making.

**Option 0: bureaucracy and rigidity**
**English Meaning:** Strict rule-based system.
**Hindi Meaning:** नौकरशाही और कठोरता
**Synonyms:** Hierarchy
**Antonyms:** Adhocracy
**Example:** The office followed bureaucracy and rigidity.

**Option 1: hierarchy and strict rules**
**English Meaning:** Top-down control with fixed rules.
**Hindi Meaning:** पदानुक्रम और सख्त नियम
**Synonyms:** Rigidity
**Antonyms:** Adhocracy
**Example:** The company ran on hierarchy and strict rules.

**Option 2: flexibility and informality** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** A flexible and informal system of management that encourages quick decisions and innovation.
**Hindi Meaning:** अव्यवस्थित संगठन, लचीली कार्यप्रणाली
**Synonyms:** Decentralization, fluidity
**Antonyms:** Bureaucracy, hierarchy
**Example:** Startups often thrive in an adhocracy that encourages innovation and quick decision-making.

**Option 3: centralization and control**
**English Meaning:** Power concentrated at the top.
**Hindi Meaning:** केंद्रीकरण और नियंत्रण
**Synonyms:** Hierarchy
**Antonyms:** Adhocracy
**Example:** The system followed centralization and control.

**Option 4: formal structure**
**English Meaning:** Official and rigid setup.
**Hindi Meaning:** औपचारिक संरचना
**Synonyms:** Bureaucracy
**Antonyms:** Adhocracy
**Example:** They preferred a formal structure.

The word means flexible and informal management. 'Flexibility and informality' is the closest synonym.

questions.text: Choose the antonym of 'Outpace':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Outpace**

**English Meaning:** To progress or develop faster than others; to surpass in speed or growth.
**Hindi Meaning:** आगे निकल जाना, पीछे छोड़ देना
**Synonyms:** Surpass, outstrip, exceed, overtake, outrun
**Antonyms:** Lag, fall behind, trail
**Example:** Technological advancements continue to outpace regulatory frameworks.

**Option 0: surpass**
**English Meaning:** To go beyond.
**Hindi Meaning:** आगे निकलना
**Synonyms:** Exceed
**Antonyms:** Lag
**Example:** Sales will surpass targets.

**Option 1: outstrip**
**English Meaning:** To leave behind.
**Hindi Meaning:** पीछे छोड़ देना
**Synonyms:** Overtake
**Antonyms:** Lag
**Example:** Demand will outstrip supply.

**Option 2: lag** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** To fall behind.
**Hindi Meaning:** पीछे रह जाना
**Synonyms:** Fall behind, trail
**Antonyms:** Outpace
**Example:** Technological advancements continue to outpace regulatory frameworks.

**Option 3: exceed**
**English Meaning:** To go over a limit.
**Hindi Meaning:** अधिक होना
**Synonyms:** Surpass
**Antonyms:** Lag
**Example:** Growth will exceed expectations.

**Option 4: overtake**
**English Meaning:** To catch up and pass.
**Hindi Meaning:** ओवरटेक करना
**Synonyms:** Outstrip
**Antonyms:** Lag
**Example:** The new company will overtake rivals.

The question asks for the antonym. 'Lag' is the opposite of 'Outpace'.

questions.text:
Choose the word that best fits the blank:
The _____ impact of small policy changes can significantly influence long-term outcomes.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Cumulative** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** Increasing by successive additions; building up over time.
**Hindi Meaning:** संचयी, क्रमिक रूप से बढ़ने वाला
**Synonyms:** Accumulated, aggregate, incremental, progressive
**Antonyms:** Decreasing, diminishing, reducing
**Example:** The cumulative impact of small policy changes can significantly influence long-term outcomes.

**Option 0: decreasing**
**English Meaning:** Becoming less.
**Hindi Meaning:** घटता हुआ
**Synonyms:** Diminishing
**Antonyms:** Cumulative
**Example:** The decreasing trend worried economists.

**Option 1: diminishing**
**English Meaning:** Getting smaller gradually.
**Hindi Meaning:** कम होता हुआ
**Synonyms:** Reducing
**Antonyms:** Cumulative
**Example:** Diminishing returns affected profits.

**Option 2: cumulative** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Increasing by successive additions; building up over time.
**Hindi Meaning:** संचयी, क्रमिक रूप से बढ़ने वाला
**Synonyms:** Accumulated, incremental
**Antonyms:** Decreasing, diminishing
**Example:** The cumulative impact of small policy changes can significantly influence long-term outcomes.

**Option 3: reducing**
**English Meaning:** Making smaller.
**Hindi Meaning:** घटाता हुआ
**Synonyms:** Diminishing
**Antonyms:** Cumulative
**Example:** The reducing cost helped the company.

**Option 4: short-term**
**English Meaning:** For a brief period.
**Hindi Meaning:** अल्पकालिक
**Synonyms:** Temporary
**Antonyms:** Cumulative
**Example:** They focused on short-term gains.

The sentence refers to effects building over time. Only 'cumulative' fits.

questions.text: Choose the closest synonym for 'Concomitant':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Concomitant** (the correct synonym is 'accompanying and associated')

**English Meaning:** Naturally accompanying something else; happening together.
**Hindi Meaning:** साथ-साथ होने वाला, संबद्ध
**Synonyms:** Accompanying, associated, attendant, linked, related
**Antonyms:** Separate, independent, unrelated
**Example:** Economic growth often brings concomitant environmental challenges.

**Option 0: separate and unrelated**
**English Meaning:** Not connected.
**Hindi Meaning:** अलग और असंबद्ध
**Synonyms:** Independent
**Antonyms:** Concomitant
**Example:** The issues were separate and unrelated.

**Option 1: independent and isolated**
**English Meaning:** Standing alone.
**Hindi Meaning:** स्वतंत्र और अलग
**Synonyms:** Unrelated
**Antonyms:** Concomitant
**Example:** The problems remained independent and isolated.

**Option 2: accompanying and associated** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Naturally accompanying something else; happening together.
**Hindi Meaning:** साथ-साथ होने वाला, संबद्ध
**Synonyms:** Attendant, linked
**Antonyms:** Separate, unrelated
**Example:** Economic growth often brings concomitant environmental challenges.

**Option 3: disconnected**
**English Meaning:** Not linked.
**Hindi Meaning:** असंबद्ध
**Synonyms:** Unrelated
**Antonyms:** Concomitant
**Example:** The events were completely disconnected.

**Option 4: standalone**
**English Meaning:** Independent and alone.
**Hindi Meaning:** स्वतंत्र
**Synonyms:** Separate
**Antonyms:** Concomitant
**Example:** The module was designed as standalone.

The word means naturally occurring together. 'Accompanying and associated' is the closest synonym.

questions.text:
Choose the word that best fits the blank:
Investor sentiment remained _____ despite short-term market volatility.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Ebullient** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** Cheerful and energetic; full of high spirits and enthusiasm.
**Hindi Meaning:** उत्साही, जोशीला
**Synonyms:** Enthusiastic, lively, exuberant, buoyant, vivacious
**Antonyms:** Dull, gloomy, subdued
**Example:** Investor sentiment remained ebullient despite short-term market volatility.

**Option 0: dull**
**English Meaning:** Lacking interest or energy.
**Hindi Meaning:** नीरस
**Synonyms:** Gloomy
**Antonyms:** Ebullient
**Example:** The mood was dull after the news.

**Option 1: gloomy**
**English Meaning:** Sad or pessimistic.
**Hindi Meaning:** उदास
**Synonyms:** Subdued
**Antonyms:** Ebullient
**Example:** The market turned gloomy.

**Option 2: ebullient** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Cheerful and energetic; full of high spirits and enthusiasm.
**Hindi Meaning:** उत्साही, जोशीला
**Synonyms:** Lively, exuberant
**Antonyms:** Dull, gloomy
**Example:** Investor sentiment remained ebullient despite short-term market volatility.

**Option 3: subdued**
**English Meaning:** Quiet and restrained.
**Hindi Meaning:** संयमित
**Synonyms:** Gloomy
**Antonyms:** Ebullient
**Example:** The team was subdued after the loss.

**Option 4: pessimistic**
**English Meaning:** Expecting the worst.
**Hindi Meaning:** निराशावादी
**Synonyms:** Gloomy
**Antonyms:** Ebullient
**Example:** He remained pessimistic about the future.

The sentence describes positive and energetic sentiment. Only 'ebullient' fits.

questions.text: Choose the antonym of 'Clamor':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Clamor**

**English Meaning:** Loud noise or strong public protest; a loud outcry.
**Hindi Meaning:** शोर, जोरदार विरोध
**Synonyms:** Uproar, outcry, protest, clamour, hue and cry
**Antonyms:** Silence, calm, quiet
**Example:** There was widespread clamor for accountability after the policy failure.

**Option 0: uproar**
**English Meaning:** Loud and angry noise.
**Hindi Meaning:** हंगामा
**Synonyms:** Outcry
**Antonyms:** Silence
**Example:** The announcement caused uproar.

**Option 1: outcry**
**English Meaning:** Strong public protest.
**Hindi Meaning:** जोरदार विरोध
**Synonyms:** Protest
**Antonyms:** Silence
**Example:** There was an outcry against the decision.

**Option 2: silence** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Complete quiet.
**Hindi Meaning:** शांति
**Synonyms:** Calm, quiet
**Antonyms:** Clamor
**Example:** There was widespread clamor for accountability after the policy failure.

**Option 3: protest**
**English Meaning:** Strong objection.
**Hindi Meaning:** विरोध
**Synonyms:** Hue and cry
**Antonyms:** Silence
**Example:** The protest grew louder.

**Option 4: hue and cry**
**English Meaning:** Loud public outcry.
**Hindi Meaning:** शोर-शराबा
**Synonyms:** Uproar
**Antonyms:** Silence
**Example:** There was hue and cry over the issue.

The question asks for the antonym. 'Silence' is the opposite of 'Clamor'.

questions.text:
One-word substitute:
An unconventional style of living.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Bohemian** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** An unconventional style of living.
**Hindi Meaning:** बोहेमियन (अपरंपरागत जीवन शैली)
**Example:** Her bohemian spirit led her to live in a commune of wandering artists.

**Option 0: Cacographer**
**English Meaning:** One who is bad in spellings.
**Hindi Meaning:** खराब वर्तनी वाला
**Example:** The professor was a self-confessed cacographer.

**Option 1: Cannibal**
**English Meaning:** One who feeds on human flesh.
**Hindi Meaning:** नरभक्षी
**Example:** The tribe was feared as ritualistic cannibals.

**Option 2: Chauvinist**
**English Meaning:** A person blindly devoted to an idea or exaggerated patriotism.
**Hindi Meaning:** कट्टर देशभक्त
**Example:** His chauvinist rhetoric alienated allies.

**Option 3: Bohemian** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** An unconventional style of living.
**Hindi Meaning:** बोहेमियन (अपरंपरागत जीवन शैली)
**Example:** Her bohemian spirit led her to live in a commune of wandering artists.

**Option 4: Auditor**
**English Meaning:** One who examines accounts.
**Hindi Meaning:** लेखा परीक्षक
**Example:** The auditor found several errors.

The exact one-word substitute for 'An unconventional style of living' is 'Bohemian'.

questions.text:
One-word substitute:
One who is bad in spellings.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Cacographer** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** One who is bad in spellings.
**Hindi Meaning:** खराब वर्तनी वाला
**Example:** The professor was a self-confessed cacographer whose notes were impossible to read.

**Option 0: Bohemian**
**English Meaning:** An unconventional style of living.
**Hindi Meaning:** बोहेमियन
**Example:** Her bohemian spirit led her to live in a commune.

**Option 1: Cannibal**
**English Meaning:** One who feeds on human flesh.
**Hindi Meaning:** नरभक्षी
**Example:** The tribe was feared as cannibals.

**Option 2: Chauvinist**
**English Meaning:** A person blindly devoted to an idea.
**Hindi Meaning:** कट्टर देशभक्त
**Example:** His chauvinist rhetoric alienated allies.

**Option 3: Cacographer** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** One who is bad in spellings.
**Hindi Meaning:** खराब वर्तनी वाला
**Example:** The professor was a self-confessed cacographer whose notes were impossible to read.

**Option 4: Atheist**
**English Meaning:** One who does not believe in God.
**Hindi Meaning:** नास्तिक
**Example:** The atheist requested a civil ceremony.

The exact one-word substitute for 'One who is bad in spellings' is 'Cacographer'.

questions.text:
One-word substitute:
One who feeds on human flesh.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Cannibal** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** One who feeds on human flesh.
**Hindi Meaning:** नरभक्षी
**Example:** The tribe was feared for their historical reputation as ritualistic cannibals.

**Option 0: Bohemian**
**English Meaning:** An unconventional style of living.
**Hindi Meaning:** बोहेमियन
**Example:** Her bohemian spirit led her to live in a commune.

**Option 1: Cacographer**
**English Meaning:** One who is bad in spellings.
**Hindi Meaning:** खराब वर्तनी वाला
**Example:** The professor was a self-confessed cacographer.

**Option 2: Chauvinist**
**English Meaning:** A person blindly devoted to an idea.
**Hindi Meaning:** कट्टर देशभक्त
**Example:** His chauvinist rhetoric alienated allies.

**Option 3: Atheist**
**English Meaning:** One who does not believe in God.
**Hindi Meaning:** नास्तिक
**Example:** The atheist requested a civil ceremony.

**Option 4: Cannibal** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** One who feeds on human flesh.
**Hindi Meaning:** नरभक्षी
**Example:** The tribe was feared for their historical reputation as ritualistic cannibals.

The exact one-word substitute for 'One who feeds on human flesh' is 'Cannibal'.

questions.text:
One-word substitute:
A person who is blindly devoted to an idea / displaying aggressive or exaggerated patriotism.
questions.solution:
Question word: **Chauvinist** (the correct fit)

**English Meaning:** A person who is blindly devoted to an idea / A person displaying aggressive or exaggerated patriotism.
**Hindi Meaning:** कट्टर देशभक्त
**Example:** His chauvinist rhetoric during the summit alienated his international allies.

**Option 0: Bohemian**
**English Meaning:** An unconventional style of living.
**Hindi Meaning:** बोहेमियन
**Example:** Her bohemian spirit led her to live in a commune.

**Option 1: Cacographer**
**English Meaning:** One who is bad in spellings.
**Hindi Meaning:** खराब वर्तनी वाला
**Example:** The professor was a self-confessed cacographer.

**Option 2: Cannibal**
**English Meaning:** One who feeds on human flesh.
**Hindi Meaning:** नरभक्षी
**Example:** The tribe was feared as cannibals.

**Option 3: Atheist**
**English Meaning:** One who does not believe in God.
**Hindi Meaning:** नास्तिक
**Example:** The atheist requested a civil ceremony.

**Option 4: Chauvinist** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** A person who is blindly devoted to an idea / A person displaying aggressive or exaggerated patriotism.
**Hindi Meaning:** कट्टर देशभक्त
**Example:** His chauvinist rhetoric during the summit alienated his international allies.

The exact one-word substitute for 'A person who is blindly devoted to an idea / displaying aggressive or exaggerated patriotism' is 'Chauvinist'.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the phrasal verb 'Lay down':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Lay down** (the correct meaning is 'Establish rules or surrender arms')

**English Meaning:** Establish rules / surrender arms.
**Hindi Meaning:** नियम स्थापित करना / हथियार समर्पण करना
**Example:** The government laid down strict laws.

**Option 0: Arrange or explain clearly**
**English Meaning:** To present in an organized way.
**Hindi Meaning:** व्यवस्थित रूप से रखना
**Example:** The architect laid out the plan.

**Option 1: Dismiss from job**
**English Meaning:** To fire employees.
**Hindi Meaning:** नौकरी से निकालना
**Example:** The company laid off many workers.

**Option 2: Establish rules or surrender arms** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Establish rules / surrender arms.
**Hindi Meaning:** नियम स्थापित करना / हथियार समर्पण करना
**Example:** The government laid down strict laws.

**Option 3: Save or abandon**
**English Meaning:** To keep aside or give up.
**Hindi Meaning:** अलग रखना या त्यागना
**Example:** Lay aside money for emergencies.

**Option 4: Propose formally**
**English Meaning:** To put forward officially.
**Hindi Meaning:** औपचारिक प्रस्ताव रखना
**Example:** A new plan was brought forward.

The phrasal verb 'Lay down' means to establish rules or surrender arms.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the phrasal verb 'Lay out':
questions.solution:
Question word: **Lay out** (the correct meaning is 'Arrange or explain clearly')

**English Meaning:** Arrange / explain clearly.
**Hindi Meaning:** व्यवस्थित रूप से रखना / स्पष्ट रूप से समझाना
**Example:** The architect laid out the plan.

**Option 0: Establish rules or surrender arms**
**English Meaning:** To set down rules.
**Hindi Meaning:** नियम स्थापित करना
**Example:** The government laid down strict laws.

**Option 1: Dismiss from job**
**English Meaning:** To fire employees.
**Hindi Meaning:** नौकरी से निकालना
**Example:** The company laid off many workers.

**Option 2: Arrange or explain clearly** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Arrange / explain clearly.
**Hindi Meaning:** व्यवस्थित रूप से रखना / स्पष्ट रूप से समझाना
**Example:** The architect laid out the plan.

**Option 3: Save or abandon**
**English Meaning:** To keep aside or give up.
**Hindi Meaning:** अलग रखना या त्यागना
**Example:** Lay aside money for emergencies.

**Option 4: Propose formally**
**English Meaning:** To put forward officially.
**Hindi Meaning:** औपचारिक प्रस्ताव रखना
**Example:** A new plan was brought forward.

The phrasal verb 'Lay out' means to arrange or explain clearly.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom 'In the public eye':
options: A question when someone is silent
Unable to speak due to nervousness
To stop yourself from saying something
questions.solution:
Question word: **In the public eye** (the correct meaning is 'Being famous or widely known')

**English Meaning:** Being famous or widely known.
**Hindi Meaning:** सार्वजनिक नजर में
**Example:** Celebrities live constantly in the public eye.

**Option 0: To agree fully**
**English Meaning:** To share the same opinion.
**Hindi Meaning:** पूरी तरह सहमत होना
**Example:** The two leaders finally saw eye to eye.

**Option 1: A question when someone is silent**
**English Meaning:** Asked when someone is unusually quiet.
**Hindi Meaning:** जुबान पर ताला क्यों?
**Example:** Why are you so quiet? Cat got your tongue?

**Option 2: Being famous or widely known** (Correct)
**English Meaning:** Being famous or widely known.
**Hindi Meaning:** सार्वजनिक नजर में
**Example:** Celebrities live constantly in the public eye.

**Option 3: Unable to speak due to nervousness**
**English Meaning:** Too shy or nervous to speak.
**Hindi Meaning:** बोलने में हिचक
**Example:** He became tongue-tied during the interview.

**Option 4: To stop yourself from saying something**
**English Meaning:** To prevent yourself from speaking.
**Hindi Meaning:** जुबान पर लगाम लगाना
**Example:** I had to bite my tongue to avoid arguing.

The idiom 'In the public eye' means being famous or widely known.
